channel.title: GrantOtter — Federal Funding Updates
channel.description: Aggregated weekly feed of grant opportunities and agency news from NIH, NSF, and DARPA.

item.title: Podcast: Onboard autonomous IndyCar racing (part 2)
item.description: Madhur Behl, an NSF-supported associate professor, discusses software challenges in autonomous racing and how his program is helping to develop the AI workforce of the future...

item.title: Looking for Animal Welfare Content? OLAW has moved to NIH Grants and Funding!
item.description: Information and other web resources from the NIH Office of Laboratory Animal Welfare (OLAW) have recently migrated to the NIH Grants and Funding hub.

item.title: Implementing SimplerNOFO at NIH: A Clearer, Simpler Path for Applicants
item.description: NIH has released its first Notice of Funding Opportunity (NOFO) under the new Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) SimplerNOFO initiative, which aims to make funding opportunities more accessible, understandable, and actionable for applicants.

item.title: Reinforcing NIH’s Continued Commitment to Fundamental Research
item.description: NIH’s policies and funding approaches are designed to maintain strong support for investigator-initiated fundamental research. NIH’s Gold Standard Science plan explains that our priority-setting processes provide strong support for hypothesis-driven research while balancing opportunities to support other forms of research. Advancing foundational knowledge of human health and disease is a stated goal for the next NIH-wide strategic plan. Here we discuss NIH’s continued commitment in this area.

item.title: Office-wide BAA: Multi X Office
item.description: Proposed research should investigate innovative approaches that enable revolutionary advances in science, devices, or systems. | See MXO

item.title: What’s in a name? At DARPA, reflecting enduring mission, future focus.
item.description: DARPA has renamed two of its technical offices.

item.title: Notice of Change to Key Dates for PAR-25-182 - Development of Psychosocial Therapeutic and Preventive Interventions for Mental Disorders (R61/R33 Clinical Trial Required)
item.description: Notice NOT-MH-26-030 from the NIH Guide for Grants and Contracts

item.title: Robotic Servicing of Geosynchronous Satellites technology to launch in 2026
item.description: DARPA and its partners approach the most significant milestone of the Robotic Servicing of Geosynchronous Satellites (RSGS) program.

item.title: A SMART approach to securing strategic materials
item.description: DARPA and State of Utah have entered into an agreement to establish the Strategic Materials Accelerator and Research Test Bed (SMART).

item.title: Notice to Rescind NOT-MH-22-310, Notice of Information on NIMH Policy Updates for Career Development Programs (K awards)
item.description: Notice NOT-MH-26-035 from the NIH Guide for Grants and Contracts

item.title: Native Collective Research Effort to Enhance Wellness (N CREW) Program: Addressing Overdose, Substance Use, Mental Health, and Pain Native Research Resource Partners
item.description: Notice NOT-DA-26-011 from the NIH Guide for Grants and Contracts

item.title: Native Collective Research Effort to Enhance Wellness (N CREW) Program: Addressing Overdose, Substance Use, Mental Health, and Pain Advancing Native Research Projects
item.description: Notice NOT-DA-26-012 from the NIH Guide for Grants and Contracts

item.title: Notice of NIDCR Pre-Application Webinar and FAQs for Community-Based Participatory Research Initiative (PAR-27-015 and RFA-DE-27-001)
item.description: Notice NOT-DE-26-014 from the NIH Guide for Grants and Contracts

item.title: Podcast: Autonomous IndyCar racing (part 1)
item.description: Artificial intelligence techniques are driving the development of autonomous vehicles. Madhur Behl, an NSF-supported associate professor, discusses how the high-speed world of racing helps advance...
